description: Pre-Session B classification for grief (reg 71). Anchor verses already set (2Co 7:10; Mat 5:4) — confirmed after reading all 485 verses. Extracts 7 None-status terms with verse data: H7451C ra.ah (disaster/harm, 152v), G3076 lupeō (to grieve, 21v), H3708B ka.a.s (vexation, 26v), H6087A a.tsav (to hurt, 15v), H3015 ya.gon (sorrow, 14v), H3511 ke.ev (pain, 6v), G3601 odunē (anguish, 2v). Reviews None-status terms without verses. Confirms candidate_delete for bleed terms.

operations.description: H7451C ra.ah [distress: harm] → extracted. Major OT distress/harm/disaster term. 152 verses. The ra.ah vocabulary covers th

set.status_note: Major OT distress/harm/disaster term. 152 verses. The ra.ah vocabulary covers the objective disaster/calamity that causes grief — it is the grievous circumstance rather than the inner response. Key uses: God 'relented from the ra.ah' (Exo 32:14; Jon 3:10; 4:2), suggesting ra.ah is the divinely-withh

operations.description: G3076 lupeō [to grieve] → extracted. THE primary NT grief verb. 56 occurrences, 21 verses. Covers: sorrow at loss (Ma

set.status_note: THE primary NT grief verb. 56 occurrences, 21 verses. Covers: sorrow at loss (Mat 17:23 — disciples distressed at death prediction), sorrowful departure (Mat 19:22 — rich young man), Gethsemane grief (Mat 26:37 — Jesus 'sorrowful and troubled'), Paul's grief-causing letter (2Co 2:2-5), and criticall

operations.description: H3708B ka.a.s [vexation] → extracted. Overlapping sub-gloss with H3708A ka.as (already extracted, 22v). Together they

set.status_note: Overlapping sub-gloss with H3708A ka.as (already extracted, 22v). Together they form the ka.as/ka.a.s vexation cluster: grief as bitter aggravation and provocation. 1Sa 1:6-16 — Hannah's rival provoking her grievously; Hannah's 'great anxiety and vexation' (ka.a.s). Psa 6:7 — 'my eye wastes away bec

operations.description: H6087A a.tsav [to hurt] → extracted. Critical term: Gen 6:6 — 'it grieved him (a.tsav) to his heart' — GOD GRIEVED at

set.status_note: Critical term: Gen 6:6 — 'it grieved him (a.tsav) to his heart' — GOD GRIEVED at humanity's wickedness. This is the divine grief anchor for the entire registry. Also: Psa 78:40 — 'they grieved him in the desert'; Isa 63:10 — 'they rebelled and grieved his Holy Spirit' (a.tsav + ru.ach qad.sho — the

operations.description: H3015 ya.gon [sorrow] → extracted. Sorrow as the sustained inner state of grief. Gen 42:38, 44:31 — Jacob's 'grey h

set.status_note: Sorrow as the sustained inner state of grief. Gen 42:38, 44:31 — Jacob's 'grey hairs brought down in sorrow to Sheol' — the most poignant grief-image in the patriarchal narrative. Jer 8:18 — 'my joy is gone; grief (ya.gon) is upon me.' Isa 35:10; 51:11 — the eschatological promise: 'sorrow and sighi

operations.description: H3511 ke.ev [pain] → extracted. Grief as bodily-felt pain. Job 2:13 — the seven-day silence before Job's pain, a

set.status_note: Grief as bodily-felt pain. Job 2:13 — the seven-day silence before Job's pain, a benchmark for how to sit with those in ke.ev. Job 16:6 — 'if I speak, my pain is not assuaged.' Psa 39:2 — 'my distress grew worse' (ke.ev). Isa 65:14 — 'cry out for pain of heart.' Jer 15:18 — 'why is my pain unceasing

operations.description: G3601 odunē [anguish] → extracted. Anguish as unceasing grief. Only 2 verses but both critical: Rom 9:2 — 'I have g

set.status_note: Anguish as unceasing grief. Only 2 verses but both critical: Rom 9:2 — 'I have great sorrow and unceasing anguish (odunē) in my heart' — Paul's grief over Israel. 1Ti 6:10 — 'through this craving [for money] some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many pangs (odynais).' An

operations.description: H0060 e.vel [mourning] → extracted_thin. Noun form: mourning. 24 occurrences, 0 verses in export. Related to H0056 a.val

set.status_note: Noun form: mourning. 24 occurrences, 0 verses in export. Related to H0056 a.val (to mourn) and H0057 a.vel (mourning). Adequately covered by H0056/H0057 verse analysis but the noun deserves registration.

operations.description: G4726 stenagmos [groan] → extracted_thin. Noun form of G4727 stenazō (already extracted, 6v). 23 occurrences. The groan as

set.status_note: Noun form of G4727 stenazō (already extracted, 6v). 23 occurrences. The groan as a communicative act — Act 7:34 (God heard Israel's groanings in Egypt). Rom 8:26 uses it for the Spirit's intercession.

operations.description: G3600 odunaō [be anguished] → extracted_thin. Verbal form of G3601 odunē (extracted above). 12 occurrences. Luk 2:48 — parents

set.status_note: Verbal form of G3601 odunē (extracted above). 12 occurrences. Luk 2:48 — parents' anguish at losing Jesus; Luk 16:24,25 — Dives in torment. Act 20:38 — grieving at Paul's departure.

operations.description: Set remaining None-status terms → delete. Bleed vocabulary: H7451I/A ra.ah sub-glosses (evil/bad — not grief-specific), H7489A ra.a (be evil), H6041 a.ni (poor/afflicted — primary in humility/anguish), H3707 ka.as (to provoke — primary in anger), H5999 a.mal (trouble/labour), H4751 mar (bitter — general bitter taste), H6035 a.nav (poor/humble — primary in humility), G5604 ōdin (birth labour), H7455 ro.a (evil), H6089A e.tsev (toil — root shared with H6094 ats.tse.vet already extracted), H6038 a.na.vah (gentleness — primary in humility registry).

Confirm candidate_delete → delete for 32 terms. Includes: H7451H/B ra sub-glosses (evil/bad general), H6031B a.nah (to afflict — primary in anguish/humility), H2470H/B/A cha.lah sub-glosses (ill/weak/beg — sickness vocabulary covered by H2470I grieved), H4753 mor (myrrh), H5998 a.mal (to toil), H7489B ra.a (to shatter), G7929 katastenazō (to groan — adequately covered by G4727 stenazō), H6001B a.mel (laborious), H4245B ma.cha.lah / H4251 ma.cha.luy (sickness), H4846 me.ro.rah (gall), H8563 tam.rur (bitterness — adequately covered by H4843 ma.rar).

set.exclusion_reason: Confirmed candidate_delete. Bleed or adequately covered by extracted terms.

summary.anchor_confirmed: 2Co 7:10 (primary — two-forms structure); Mat 5:4 (secondary — beatitude of grief)
summary.key_finding: H6087A a.tsav — Gen 6:6 (God grieved) and Isa 63:10 (they grieved his Holy Spirit) — the divine grief anchor for the registry and the OT precedent for Eph 4:30 (do not grieve the Holy Spirit).
